message:
test(time): classify_vdso_return boundary + errno invariant (bd-afqt)

Cod's bd-1isd fix (commit 7440043) added classify_vdso_return with 3
test cases (rc=7, rc=-EINVAL, rc=-ENOSYS). Add 3 complementary tests:

vdso_classify_zero_is_success
  Pins the happy-path Success branch — rc=0 is the only input that
  must yield Success. A refactor that merged Success into one of the
  Fallback arms would be caught here.

vdso_classify_boundary_positive_and_negative_rc
  rc=1 (smallest positive) and rc=i32::MAX exercise the "positive →
  FallbackToSyscall" arm at both ends. rc=-1 (smallest-magnitude
  negative that isn't -ENOSYS) guards against a regression that would
  flip the "negative and not -ENOSYS" guard into a fallback and leak
  real vDSO errors.

vdso_classify_fail_errno_is_always_positive
  Structural invariant sweep: for every negative rc that isn't
  -ENOSYS, the Fail(e) outcome must have e>0 so it can legally be
  stored in errno. A bug that forgot to negate rc (or mishandled
  sign-extension) would emit a non-positive errno in violation of
  POSIX.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "bd-1hq", "title": "Kernel: Groebner normal form for violation signatures (design)", "description": "Design Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization for anomaly/violation signatures.

Goal:
- Produce stable root-cause IDs from many overlapping anomaly signals.
- Avoid double-counting and improve sparse latent-cause recovery.

Design tasks:
- Define signature variables (small integer features derived from cached states).
- Define constraints/relations and what the normal form represents.
- Choose offline artifact: Groebner basis + reduction table (no heavy algebra at runtime).

Acceptance criteria:
- Concrete signature schema + deterministic reduction procedure + output ID format.", "notes": "Drafted design doc: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner_normal_form_design.md (v1 atom schema A00..A24 + optional class vars C0..C5; relations/term order; offline artifact format; deterministic reduction; id format).", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "BrightCave", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:32:55.351807261Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T19:13:26.094075093Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T19:13:26.094056689Z", "close_reason": "Drafted crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner_normal_form_design.md with v1 schema, relations/term order, offline artifact sketch, deterministic reduction procedure, and gn1 id format.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "bd-1ik", "title": "Opportunity matrix workflow: score hotspots and enforce score>=2.0 before implementation", "description": "Extreme optimization mapping.

Deliverables:
- Implement hotspot scoring matrix with impact/confidence/effort and computed score.
- Gate optimization work so only score>=2.0 items proceed.

Acceptance:
- Optimization backlog always includes explicit score rationale.
- Low-score work remains deferred unless manually justified.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "notes": "2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): opportunity_matrix_test (7 pass) + gate script. Scoring dimensions defined, threshold enforced (>=2.0), scores match formula, entries have required fields.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CrimsonCove", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:48:12.172285695Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z", "close_reason": "Opportunity matrix workflow operational. 7 harness tests pass. check_opportunity_matrix.sh gate validates scores, thresholds, and formula consistency.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "perf", "planning"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-1ik", "depends_on_id": "bd-26o", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:28Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1isd","title":"vDSO fastpath: defensive handling for positive rc + cross-arch version strings","description":"Review of time_abi.rs vDSO fastpath (commit 92989d2) flagged two minor issues that don't affect current correctness:

1) In raw_clock_gettime (line 102-105), if vdso_clock_gettime returns an unexpected POSITIVE rc (buggy vDSO or future ABI change), the current code computes set_abi_errno(-rc) which yields a negative errno value. Per POSIX errno must be positive. Fix: guard with rc < 0 check before negating, or treat any non-{0, -ENOSYS} result as a kernel anomaly warranting fallback.

2) resolve_vdso_symbols (line 166, 178) hardcodes c"LINUX_2.6" as the dlvsym version. x86_64's vDSO uses LINUX_2.6, but aarch64 uses LINUX_2.6.39 and riscv64 uses LINUX_4.15. Currently falls back to dlsym (unversioned) on non-x86_64 — works but costs a wasted dlvsym call. Fix: per-arch version constants.

Discovered during bd-j946 review.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:08:36.457319974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:42:25.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:42:25.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-1isd","title":"vDSO fastpath: defensive handling for positive rc + cross-arch version strings","description":"Review of time_abi.rs vDSO fastpath (commit 92989d2) flagged two minor issues that don't affect current correctness:

1) In raw_clock_gettime (line 102-105), if vdso_clock_gettime returns an unexpected POSITIVE rc (buggy vDSO or future ABI change), the current code computes set_abi_errno(-rc) which yields a negative errno value. Per POSIX errno must be positive. Fix: guard with rc < 0 check before negating, or treat any non-{0, -ENOSYS} result as a kernel anomaly warranting fallback.

2) resolve_vdso_symbols (line 166, 178) hardcodes c"LINUX_2.6" as the dlvsym version. x86_64's vDSO uses LINUX_2.6, but aarch64 uses LINUX_2.6.39 and riscv64 uses LINUX_4.15. Currently falls back to dlsym (unversioned) on non-x86_64 — works but costs a wasted dlvsym call. Fix: per-arch version constants.

 {"id": "bd-1iya", "title": "bd-rqn subtask: Compile-time profile gates enforcing production-only default kernel", "description": "Background:
- Classification alone is insufficient; feature/build boundaries must enforce it.

Goal:
- Implement compile-time gating so default production builds exclude research-only controllers by construction.

Deliverables:
1) Feature flag architecture for production vs research kernels.
2) Build profile checks ensuring expected module set per profile.
3) Guard scripts to detect accidental profile drift.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Default production build contains only approved production controllers.
- Research profile can opt-in additional controllers without affecting production path.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for profile/module mapping.
- CI checks across profile combinations.
- Structured logs for build-profile validation outcomes.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "Codex", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:02:38.464980345Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-12T22:34:07.345472796Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-12T22:33:36.904979185Z", "close_reason": "Implemented runtime_math profile-gate script + compile-time enforcement + harness mapping tests + CI extended-gate hook", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["build", "ci", "critique", "math", "verification"], "comments": [{"id": 677, "issue_id": "bd-1iya", "author": "Codex", "text": "Starting bd-1iya. Plan: (1) define Cargo feature/profile split for production vs research runtime_math modules (default=production), (2) generate/validate compile-time module set vs manifest+classification matrix, (3) add gate script + harness tests to prevent drift, (4) wire into scripts/ci.sh extended gates.", "created_at": "2026-02-12T22:24:22Z"}, {"id": 678, "issue_id": "bd-1iya", "author": "Codex", "text": "Landed bd-1iya in commit 4d3bd6c.

Changes:
- Enforced  at compile time (membrane compile_error if disabled)
- Added build-profile gate: scripts/check_runtime_math_profile_gates.sh (writes JSONL logs + report)
- Added harness mapping test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/runtime_math_profile_gates_test.rs
- Wired gate into scripts/ci.sh extended gates

Validation:
- scripts/check_runtime_math_profile_gates.sh (PASS)
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_profile_gates_test (3 passed)
- cargo fmt --check (PASS)

Structured artifacts:
- target/conformance/runtime_math_profile_gates.log.jsonl
- target/conformance/runtime_math_profile_gates.report.json", "created_at": "2026-02-12T22:33:43Z"}, {"id": 679, "issue_id": "bd-1iya", "author": "Codex", "text": "Correction: enforced runtime-math-production at compile time (crate refuses to build with --no-default-features / without runtime-math-production).", "created_at": "2026-02-12T22:34:07Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-1j2u", "title": "bd-1gh subtask: pthread_key registry architecture and lifecycle semantics", "description": "Background:
- pthread_key APIs are currently blocking stub-burn-down progress and depend on thread lifecycle correctness.

Goal:
- Define and implement key-table architecture with deterministic key allocation/deallocation semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Global key registry design and limits policy.
2) Thread-local value slot mapping strategy.
3) key_create/key_delete semantics and error paths.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Key lifecycle semantics are deterministic and race-safe.
- Explicit handling for exhausted key space and invalid keys.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for key allocation/deletion and invalid operations.
- Structured logs: trace_id, key_id, op, result, errno, timing.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:00:52.416477429Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T17:34:32.794586803Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T17:34:32.794496324Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "implementation", "pthread", "testing", "tls"], "comments": [{"id": 680, "issue_id": "bd-1j2u", "author": "PurpleHawk", "text": "PurpleHawk: Key registry architecture verified and expanded with edge-case tests. Implementation in tls.rs is solid: Mutex-protected global key registry (1024 slots), open-addressed TID→values-ptr hash table (4096 slots, allocation-free), generation counter for stale key detection. Added 12 new tests: out-of-bounds keys, generation counter semantics, slot reuse with different destructors, double-teardown safety, table operations on nonexistent TIDs, 64-key independence, key_delete does not call destructors, zero-value handling. Total: 29 passing tests.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T17:34:31Z"}]}
 {"id": "bd-1j4", "title": "EPIC: Hard Parts Roadmap (rtld/CRT + NSS/locale/resolver)", "description": "Critique mapping: #4.

Goal: make the ‘hard parts’ explicit with phased milestones.

Deliverables:
- Bootstrap strategy doc (what ‘drop-in replacement’ means for us).
- Incremental plan for crt startup, loader, NSS/resolv, locale/iconv.

Acceptance:
- Each missing subsystem has a bead with acceptance tests and a measurable milestone.

Verification Mandate:
- Every implementation child bead MUST ship comprehensive unit tests and deterministic e2e scripts (strict + hardened where applicable).
- Every test/e2e execution MUST emit detailed structured logs (trace_id, mode, symbol/API family, decision path, errno/outcome, timing, and artifact pointers).
- No bead may close without: test commands, expected outputs, and failure-log artifact examples documented in bead notes or linked reports.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Success Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.", "notes": "## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.", "status": "open", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:36:07.020884278Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-11T04:28:03.314006841Z", "close_reason": "Hard Parts Roadmap: 5/9 children closed. RTLD phase-1, NSS/resolver, locale/iconv, hard-parts battery all complete. Remaining items are release-gate epics.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "locale", "nss", "resolver", "rtld"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": +{"id":"bd-afqt","title":"vDSO classify_vdso_return: boundary + invariant coverage","description":"Cod's bd-1isd fix (commit 7440043) added classify_vdso_return() with 3 test cases (rc=7, rc=-EINVAL, rc=-ENOSYS). Adds quickcheck-style sweep + explicit boundary tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:49:36.638177405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:49:44.192904231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-ahjd", "title": "bd-2ry subtask: Deterministic fixture pack for nested/edge non-local jump scenarios", "description": "Background:
- setjmp correctness must be demonstrated via integration fixtures, not just unit tests.

Goal:
- Add deterministic fixture pack for non-local control transfer, including adversarial edge cases.

Deliverables:
1) Fixture programs for nested jumps and error handling paths.
2) Strict+hardened expected-output profiles.
3) Failure artifact capture and triage docs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Fixture suite passes in declared supported scope.
- Any unsupported scenario fails with explicit documented semantics.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E scripts with deterministic replay controls.
- Structured logs: trace_id, scenario_id, mode, jump_depth, mask_state, outcome, timing, artifact_refs.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.", "notes": "## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "RusticCoast", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:01:53.128299308Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-14T08:24:17.069464742Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-14T08:24:17.069446628Z", "close_reason": "Implemented deterministic setjmp fixture pack + deterministic logging and triage artifacts", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "e2e", "logging", "setjmp", "testing"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-ahjd", "depends_on_id": "bd-2xp3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:32Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1067, "issue_id": "bd-ahjd", "author": "RusticCoast", "text": "Delivery evidence (RusticCoast):

 {"id": "bd-am9u", "title": "EPIC: Dynamic System Constants — Replace hard-coded values with runtime queries", "description": "FrankenLibC currently hard-codes several system-dependent constants (page size, stack limits, group counts, FD limits) that should be queried at runtime for cross-architecture correctness.

BACKGROUND: While FrankenLibC currently targets x86_64 Linux, these hard-coded values create latent bugs that will surface when porting to ARM64 (16KB/64KB pages), RISC-V, or systems with non-standard configurations. Even on x86_64, some values (like supplementary group count) can differ from defaults.

SCOPE: This epic covers all hard-coded constants identified in the comprehensive audit across malloc_abi.rs, unistd_abi.rs, glibc_internal_abi.rs, stdlib_abi.rs, pthread_abi.rs, and process_abi.rs.

ALREADY DONE: sysconf(_SC_PAGESIZE) now queries AT_PAGESZ from /proc/self/auxv at runtime (cached). The mmap_alloc bootstrap path intentionally keeps 4096 as the minimum alignment (documented).

RATIONALE: AGENTS.md says "We want to do things the RIGHT way with NO TECH DEBT" — hard-coded system constants are textbook tech debt.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "assignee": "PlumCove", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:03:22.060285167Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T23:03:59.322247279Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T23:03:59.321965265Z", "close_reason": "Delivered: code + artifacts present.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "bd-am9u.1", "title": "Replace hard-coded 64-group limit with dynamic NGROUPS_MAX query", "description": "FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs:4592
CURRENT: let mut groups = [0u32; 64]
PROBLEM: NGROUPS_MAX can be 65536 on modern Linux. Hard-coding 64 silently truncates group membership for users in >64 groups, breaking permission checks.
FIX: Use a two-pass approach — first call getgroups(0, NULL) to get count, then allocate dynamically. Or use sysconf(_SC_NGROUPS_MAX) to size the buffer.
IMPACT: Any program calling getgroups() or initgroups() for users in many groups will get truncated results.
CONSIDERATION: The Vec allocation here is fine since this isn't a bootstrap-critical path.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:03:51.199909661Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:11:46.904224615Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:11:46.903226145Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-am9u.1", "depends_on_id": "bd-am9u", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:03:51.199909661Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "bd-l93x.3", "title": "Benchmark gate: verify <20ns strict, <200ns hardened per symbol", "description": "Implement per-symbol benchmark gate that enforces the TSM overhead targets: strict mode <20ns overhead per invocation, hardened mode <200ns overhead per invocation. Methodology: (1) For each of 250 symbols, measure baseline latency (raw implementation without TSM). (2) Measure strict mode latency (TSM validation in fast path). (3) Measure hardened mode latency (TSM validation + quarantine checks + healing oracle). (4) Compute overhead = mode_latency - baseline_latency. (5) Assert: strict_overhead < 20ns at p99, hardened_overhead < 200ns at p99. Use criterion.rs with --measurement-time=10 for statistical significance. Pin to CPU core, disable frequency scaling, warm up caches. Store results as structured artifacts (JSON + flamegraphs). CI gate: any symbol exceeding the threshold blocks the merge with detailed regression report showing which symbol, which mode, measured overhead, and historical trend. Generate p50/p95/p99 breakdown per symbol per mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "design": "**Alien CS Reference:** Performance benchmarking methodology from Mytkowicz et al. (2009) on measurement bias. Criterion.rs statistical framework. TSM overhead budget from project specification.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- SymbolBenchmark macro: generates criterion benchmark group for each symbol with raw/strict/hardened configurations.
- CPU pinning via sched_setaffinity(). Frequency scaling disabled via cpupower frequency-set --governor performance.
- Warm-up: 1000 iterations before measurement. Measurement: 10 seconds per benchmark.
- Overhead = mode_latency - raw_latency. Assert: strict_overhead < 20ns at p99, hardened_overhead < 200ns at p99.
- Results stored as JSON with system info (CPU model from /proc/cpuinfo, kernel version from uname).", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
1. Benchmark suite covers all 250 symbols in all 3 configurations (raw, strict, hardened).
2. Strict mode overhead < 20ns at p99 for all symbols (CI gate).
3. Hardened mode overhead < 200ns at p99 for all symbols (CI gate).
4. Results reproducible: coefficient of variation < 5% across 3 consecutive runs.
5. Regression detection: any symbol exceeding 110% of baseline triggers CI failure with detailed report.
6. Flamegraph generation for top-10 overhead symbols (SVG artifacts stored).
7. Historical trend data maintained across releases for regression tracking.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "**Logging Requirements:**
- Per-symbol result: JSON with symbol, family, mode, p50_ns, p95_ns, p99_ns, overhead_ns, raw_baseline_ns.
- CI gate output: pass/fail per symbol with threshold and measured value.
- Regression report: symbol, previous_p99, current_p99, delta_pct, verdict.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "Codex", "created_at": "2026-02-13T09:22:48.408462937Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T22:40:55.473803753Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T22:40:55.473602331Z", "close_reason": "Added CI perf-gate wiring coverage for the benchmark gate path, including elimination and metadata-read gate checks (ci_perf_gate_wiring_test.rs and related benchmark-gate scripts), and verified with rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test ci_perf_gate_wiring_test.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-l93x.3", "depends_on_id": "bd-3h1u.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-l93x.3", "depends_on_id": "bd-l93x", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-l93x.4", "title": "Healing oracle: verify hardened mode repair for each symbol", "description": "Implement the healing oracle verification framework that validates hardened mode's repair behavior for each symbol. For every symbol, the healing oracle must verify: (1) Detection — when a fault is injected (UAF pointer, double-free, buffer overflow, invalid argument), the TSM membrane detects it and transitions from 'safe' to 'suspicious' or 'unsafe' state. (2) Repair — in hardened mode, the TSM applies the correct repair action: quarantine (for UAF/double-free), safe default return (for invalid arguments), truncation (for buffer overflows), errno propagation (for system errors). (3) Correctness — the repaired behavior is a valid POSIX-compatible response (not arbitrary). (4) Evidence — the evidence ledger records the detection, classification, repair action, and outcome. Test matrix: for each symbol, generate fault injection vectors targeting each TSM state transition. Verify all transitions produce correct repairs. Generate a conformance report: symbol x fault_type -> detected/repaired/evidence_logged.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "design": "**Alien CS Reference:** Fault injection testing from Natella et al. (2016). Healing/self-repair from Rinard et al. (2004) failure-oblivious computing. Applied to TSM hardened-mode repair verification.

**Rust Implementation Guidance:**
- HealingOracle<S: Symbol>: generic oracle that injects fault, invokes symbol, verifies repair outcome.
- FaultType enum { Uaf, DoubleFree, BufferOverflow, InvalidArg, NullDeref, IntegerOverflow, FormatString }.
- For each (symbol, fault_type): inject fault via test hook, verify TSM detects (state transition logged), verify repair action is POSIX-compatible.
- RepairVerifier: check return value against POSIX error specification (e.g., NULL + EINVAL, -1 + EFAULT).
- Evidence ledger query after each test: verify detection, classification, repair, and outcome entries present.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
1. Healing oracle covers all 250 symbols x 7 fault types = 1750 test cases (some may be not-applicable).
2. Detection rate: 100% for UAF and double-free (guaranteed by generational arena).
3. Repair correctness: all repairs produce POSIX-valid return values (verified against POSIX spec table).
4. Evidence ledger completeness: every detected fault has detection + classification + repair + outcome entries.
5. No crash or undefined behavior in the repair path itself (run under ASan/MSan).
6. Healing oracle deterministic: same fault produces same repair across 100 runs.
7. Conformance report: symbol x fault_type -> detected/repaired/evidence_logged/posix_valid matrix.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "**Logging Requirements:**
- Per test: tracing::info!(target: healing_oracle, symbol, fault_type, detected, repair_action, posix_valid).
- Failures: tracing::error!(target: healing_oracle, symbol, fault_type, expected_repair, actual_result).
- Summary: healing_oracle_report.json with full matrix and aggregate statistics.

### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "StormyCardinal", "created_at": "2026-02-13T09:22:58.031589642Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T23:04:09.069706420Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T23:04:09.069471676Z", "close_reason": "Delivered: code + artifacts present.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-l93x.4", "depends_on_id": "bd-3h1u.5", "type": "related", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-l93x.4", "depends_on_id": "bd-l93x", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1111, "issue_id": "bd-l93x.4", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Progress update (BoldCedar): implemented deterministic healing-oracle report pipeline and gate scaffolding.

 {"id": "bd-ldj", "title": "Epic: Symbol Coverage Expansion - POSIX/libc ABI Surface", "description": "Drive from 250 symbols (8% of glibc's 3160) toward full coverage. Priority batches: stdio, pthread, threading, resolver/NSS, iconv/locale. Track Implemented vs RawSyscall vs GlibcCallThrough vs Stub. Per-symbol conformance fixture: capture→verify→traceability→healing oracle→benchmark gate.

## Success Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Success Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "RusticWolf 2026-02-25: Promoted 4 syslog symbols (openlog, closelog, syslog, vsyslog) from GlibcCallThrough to Implemented.  {"id": "bd-ldj.1", "title": "Symbols: stdio family implementation (fopen/fclose/fread/fwrite/fprintf/fscanf/fseek/fflush)", "description": "Implement stdio family: buffered I/O with FILE* management, format strings, seeking, flushing. See `acceptance_criteria` field.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "fmemopen+open_memstream promoted GlibcCallThrough→Implemented. Native POSIX-compliant implementations in stdio_abi.rs with memory-backed fast paths in fread/fwrite/fseek/fflush/fclose/fgetc/fputc. open_memstream sync metadata tracks C caller ptr/sizeloc, synced on flush/close via malloc+copy. 18 new unit tests (MemBacking + StdioStream memory ops), all pass. support_matrix.json updated. - Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.

Changes:
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs
  - Extended pthread_setname_np/pthread_getname_np to support non-self thread handles via /proc/self/task/<tid>/comm using resolve_thread_tid().
  - Replaced previous ENOSYS path for non-self with ESRCH-aware procfs handling.
  - Added errno-preserving failure paths for prctl() set/get operations.
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs
  - Added self roundtrip test for pthread_setname_np/pthread_getname_np.
  - Added cross-thread roundtrip test (create child thread, set/get child name, join).
  - Added unknown-thread ESRCH contract tests.

Verification (all via rch):
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check  ✅
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets  ✅
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  ✅
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture  ✅ (10 passed, 5 ignored)
